title: ～から見ると
titleEn: Express a perspective or point of view; 'from the standpoint of', 'in terms of', 'from the perspective of'.
explanation: Express a perspective or point of view; 'from the standpoint of', 'in terms of', 'from the perspective of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～から見ると grammar point is used to express a particular perspective or point of view. In English, it translates to 'from the standpoint of', 'from the perspective of', or 'in terms of'. It is usually used with a noun that indicates the perspective.

title: ～から言うと
titleEn: Express a judgment or opinion based on a specific criterion; 'speaking from', 'in terms of', 'from the viewpoint of'.
structure: Noun + から言うと (common), [Verb-casual / Adjective + から言って] (less common)
structureEn: Noun + から言うと (common), [Verb-casual / Adjective + から言って] (less common)
explanation: Express a judgment or opinion based on a specific criterion; 'speaking from', 'in terms of', 'from the viewpoint of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～から言うと grammar point is used to express a judgment or opinion based on a specific criterion or aspect. It can be translated as 'speaking from', 'in terms of', or 'from the viewpoint of' in English. It is most commonly used with nouns, although you may occasionally see other forms (like adjectives).

title: ～くせに
titleEn: Used to criticize someone for doing something contrary to what one would expect from their nature or situation.
structure: Verb (casual) + くせに / い-Adjective + くせに / な-Adjective + な(or の) + くせに / Noun + の + くせに
structureEn: Verb (casual) + くせに / い-Adjective + くせに / な-Adjective + な(or の) + くせに / Noun + の + くせに
explanation: Used to criticize someone for doing something contrary to what one would expect from their nature or situation. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～くせに is used to express criticism or disapproval toward someone who does something contradictory to their expected nature, role, or prior statement. It's used in fairly casual or spoken contexts and can sound quite critical or accusing. Note that there's a colloquial variant ～くせして, but ～くせに is the standard form taught in most references.

title: ～ことから
titleEn: Indicates the reason or basis for something; 'because', 'since', 'due to'.
structure: Verb-casual + ことから / い-Adjective + ことから / な-Adjective + なことから / Noun + のことから
structureEn: Verb-casual + ことから / い-Adjective + ことから / な-Adjective + なことから / Noun + のことから
explanation: Indicates the reason or basis for something; 'because', 'since', 'due to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ことから grammar point is used to indicate the reason or basis for a conclusion, fact, or premise. It can be translated as 'because', 'since', or 'due to' in English. The same pattern is used whether it’s with verbs, adjectives, or nouns (with the appropriate こと or の).

title: ～ことに
titleEn: Used to express feelings or emotions about a situation, action, or outcome.
structure: Verb-casual + ことに / い-Adjective + ことに / な-Adjective + なことに / Noun + のことに
structureEn: Verb-casual + ことに / い-Adjective + ことに / な-Adjective + なことに / Noun + のことに
explanation: Used to express feelings or emotions about a situation, action, or outcome. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ことに grammar point is used to highlight the speaker’s feelings or emotional reaction toward a situation, action, or outcome. It often appears in constructions like 驚いたことに, 残念なことに, 不思議なことに, etc.

title: ～ことになっている
titleEn: Expresses that something is supposed to be or has been decided (rule, plan, custom).
structure: Verb-plain form + ことになっている / い-Adjective + ことになっている / な-Adjective + だ + ことになっている / Noun + だ + ことになっている
structureEn: Verb-plain form + ことになっている / い-Adjective + ことになっている / な-Adjective + だ + ことになっている / Noun + だ + ことになっている
explanation: Expresses that something is supposed to be or has been decided (rule, plan, custom). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ことになっている grammar point is used when something has been decided, planned, or there is a rule/custom that everyone follows. It can be translated as 'it is supposed to be,' 'it is decided,' or 'it is customary that.'

title: ～さえ～ば
titleEn: Expresses that, as long as a certain condition is met, something else will follow; 'if only', 'provided that'.
structure: Noun + さえ + Verb-conditional / Verb-ますstem + さえすれば / etc. (varies by part of speech)
structureEn: Noun + さえ + Verb-conditional / Verb-ますstem + さえすれば / etc. (varies by part of speech)
explanation: Expresses that, as long as a certain condition is met, something else will follow; 'if only', 'provided that'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～さえ～ば grammar point is used to indicate that if a certain single condition is fulfilled, everything else will be fine or a certain result will occur. It often translates as 'as long as...' or 'if only...'.

title: ～ざるを得ない
titleEn: Have no choice but to; cannot help but.
structure: Verb-ない form (drop ない) + ざるを得ない
structureEn: Verb-ない form (drop ない) + ざるを得ない
explanation: Have no choice but to; cannot help but. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ざるを得ない grammar point is used to indicate that one has no choice but to do something or cannot avoid doing it. It is formed from the negative form (ない) but replaces ない with ざる, followed by を得ない. (Note: する → せざるを得ない.)
